bugs.title: Cauldron glow PointLight and steam particles added to group — NOT orphaned at scene root
bugs.severity: CONFIRMED-FIXED (previously reported as GHOST — audit finds group.add, not scene.add)

exact_code: L543:  const steamPoints = new THREE.Points(geo, steamMat);
evidence.analysis: Both glow PointLight and steamPoints are correctly added to the local `group` (not `scene`). When cauldron_merlin.glb loads (LairGLBAssets.ts L40), `proceduralGroups.cauldronGroup.visible = false` is called, which sets the entire group invisible — including the glow and steamPoints. BUG-L-07-GHOST as originally described (scene.add) does NOT exist in the current code. The bug has either been fixed prior to C72 or the C43 report was forward-looking. The ghost-light risk is neutralised.

bugs.verdict: CLOSED — not reproducible in current source

bugs.title: Candle particle systems added directly to scene (not to candleGroup) — orphaned when bougie.glb hides the group

exact_code: L370:  const points = new THREE.Points(geo, particleMat);
L371:  scene.add(points);   // BUG: should be group.add(points)
evidence.analysis: createCandles() correctly builds a `group` that holds candle bodies, wicks, and the shared PointLight (L322 → group.add(sharedLight)). However, each candle's flame particle system (`THREE.Points`) is added directly to `scene` at L371 instead of to `group`. When bougie.glb loads successfully (LairGLBAssets.ts L63), `candleGroup.visible = false` hides bodies+wicks+sharedLight but leaves 3 orphaned orange particle emitters floating at the candle positions in world space. This is the particle-ghost equivalent of BUG-L-07.
evidence.impact: 3 floating orange flame particle systems remain visible over the GLB bougie candles whenever the GLB asset loads. Severity: HIGH — visually breaking on any device that successfully loads bougie.glb.

bugs.recommendation: Change MerlinLairScene.ts L371 from `scene.add(points)` to `group.add(points)`. The `group` reference is in scope at that callsite.

bugs.title: Candle body mesh uses shared candleMat — no material.clone() per instance

lines.all_bodies_share_it: 328 → new THREE.Mesh(..., candleMat) — same reference for all 3 candles

exact_code: L328:  const body = new THREE.Mesh(new THREE.CylinderGeometry(0.1, 0.12, 0.7, 8), candleMat);
evidence.analysis: All three candle body meshes share the same MeshStandardMaterial instance. Any emissive hover change applied to one candle body (e.g., on pointer-enter) will mutate emissiveIntensity for all three simultaneously. The procedural fallback path does not currently implement per-candle hover on the bodies (hover is driven by the GLB visual mesh after load), but the shared material is a latent bug that will manifest if hover is extended to the procedural path or if any future pass modifies the material directly.
evidence.impact: MEDIUM — not immediately visible in GLB path, but materialises if procedural hover is ever implemented or if dispose() resets emissive on one body.

bugs.recommendation: In the candle body creation loop, use `candleMat.clone()` per iteration: `new THREE.Mesh(geo, candleMat.clone())`. Dispose individual clones in the scene teardown.

lairDensity_orphan_check.finding: One DirectionalLight target is added to scene directly (L25: scene.add(moon.target)) rather than to the density group. This is intentional and correct: DirectionalLight targets MUST be in the scene graph (world space) for lookAt to function. The moon DirectionalLight itself is in the group (L24: group.add(moon)), so it disappears correctly with the density group. No unintentional orphan lights found in LairDensity.

cauldron_return_structure_check.createCauldron_return: L567 → { group, body, glow, positions, velocities, lifetimes, geo, update }
cauldron_return_structure_check.verdict: CORRECT — group encapsulates all cauldron sub-objects. GLB hide path works as designed.

ghost_light_severity.notes: BUG-L-07 is resolved: glow + steam are in group. Minor residual risk: cauldronHit sphere is added to scene directly (L692) and is never hidden on GLB load — but it is invisible (MeshBasicMaterial visible:false) so no visual artefact.

candle_material_isolation.notes: Shared candleMat is a latent correctness issue (BUG-L-CANDLE-SHARED-MAT). Candle flame particles are orphaned from group (BUG-L-10-CANDLE-MAT, HIGH). Two independent issues in the candle system.

overall_scene_encapsulation.notes: Cauldron and all major systems are properly grouped. The candle particle orphan is the only confirmed group-escape defect. LairDensity.ts intentional scene.add for light target is acceptable. setupLighting lights are at scene root by design (global fill/key/rim). Score penalised for HIGH candle-particles bug.

top_2_bugs_summary.description: Candle flame particles added to scene not group — 3 orphaned orange particle emitters survive bougie.glb group-hide
All 3 candle bodies share one MeshStandardMaterial instance — hover emissive changes will bleed across all 3 simultaneously

c43_bug_l07_verdict: NOT REPRODUCED in current source. glow (L521) and steamPoints (L544) are both group.add(). The C43 report may have targeted a now-patched state, or described a forward risk that was addressed before it shipped.
